title: The Sea-side Doll.
content:
There's one doll for winter,
        When ice comes and snow;
    Another for spring time,
        When primroses grow.

    A dolly for dark nights,
        To take into bed;
    And one for the morning,
        Till lessons are said.

    But this is the dolly
        To play on the sands,
    You see both a pail and
        A spade in her hands.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Richard Hunter delivers a powerful performance in "The Sea-side Doll."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
